name: Dose–Response & EC50
description: Pharmacodynamic characterization: apply a range of doses, measure the response, fit a sigmoidal concentration-response (Hill) curve to extract potency (EC50/IC50) and efficacy, and assess cooperativity and the therapeutic window.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Compound + target/cells"]
    N2["Apply range of doses"]
    N3["Measure response per dose"]
    N4["Plot response vs log[dose]"]
    N5["Fit sigmoidal (Hill) curve"]
    N6["Extract EC50 / IC50"]
    N7{"Steep Hill slope?"}
    N8["Cooperative binding"]
    N9["Quantify potency/efficacy"]
    N10["Therapeutic window"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: The possible effects of the aggregation of haemoglobin on its dissociation curves (Hill equation)
International Union of Pharmacology Committee on Receptor Nomenclature and Drug Classification
sources.authors: Neubig, R. R.; Spedding, M.; Kenakin, T.; Christopoulos, A.

sources.title: Goodman & Gilman's The Pharmacological Basis of Therapeutics, 13th ed.
sources.authors: Brunton, L. L.; Hilal-Dandan, R.; Knollmann, B. C.
